title: Bramley apple cake
subtitle: A simple British apple cake with a cinnamon crumb
excerpt: A straightforward British apple cake built on a plain sponge with chunks of Bramley baked through. The apple collapses to soft pockets in the crumb and the top sets to a light crust dusted with cinnamon sugar.
sourceNotes: Apple cake in various forms runs through the British baking tradition from the seventeenth century, when fruit was folded into spiced sponge batters. The Bramley apple (a cooking variety bred in Southwell, Nottinghamshire, in the nineteenth century) became the standard for baked goods because it collapses to a soft puree in heat, giving moisture without water logging the crumb. Marguerite Patten's Everyday Cook Book (1968) and Delia Smith's Complete Cookery Course (1978) both include versions of this cake.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool completely. Wrap in cling film and freeze for up to 2 months. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps well for 3 days in an airtight tin. The apple continues to soften and the cake improves on day two.

baking.decoratingTechnique: cinnamon sugar scattered over the top before baking

content.text: Bramley apples are the right apple for this cake because they break down under heat, releasing juice into the surrounding sponge and collapsing to soft pockets. An eating apple holds its shape too well and gives a different, drier result. Peel, core, and chop the apple into pieces of roughly 2 cm: too fine and they vanish; too large and they sink.

Test for doneness with a skewer pushed into the centre away from a piece of apple. The apple itself will always feel soft; you want the sponge around it to be clean. Cover the top loosely with foil in the last 10 minutes if it colours faster than expected.

attrs.prepNote: peeled, cored, cut into 2 cm pieces (about 2 medium apples)

content.text: Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Grease and line a 23 cm round cake tin. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{caster-sugar}} together until pale and fluffy, about 4 minutes. Beat in the {{eggs}} one at a time. Fold in the {{plain-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{cinnamon-ground}} in two additions, then stir in the {{whole-milk}}. Fold in the {{apple-bramley}} pieces. Spoon into the prepared tin and level the top.

Mix the {{demerara-sugar}} with the extra {{cinnamon-ground}} and scatter over the top. Bake for 42 to 48 minutes until a skewer inserted in the centre sponge (not into a piece of apple) comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes before turning out onto a rack. Serve warm or at room temperature.
